atomSequence.intro: Here we decide only the **sign** of the answer, not its size. The rule: same signs give a positive, different signs give a negative.

items.label: N
items.note: Different signs (one positive, one negative) → the answer is negative, not positive.

items.label: P
items.note: Flip one sign: now both are negative. Same signs → positive.

items.label: P
items.note: The rule works for division too. Both negative → same signs → positive.

items.label: P
items.note: Two positives also count as 'same signs' → positive.

items.label: N
items.note: Change one sign back: different signs → negative.

mastery.q: Without computing, is $(-2) \times (-3) \times (-1)$ positive or negative?
mastery.a: Negative — three negative factors (an odd number of negatives) gives a negative.

mastery.q: Without computing, is $(-1) \times (-2) \times (-3) \times (-4)$ positive or negative?
mastery.a: Positive — four negatives (an even number) gives a positive.

mastery.q: Without computing, is $(-5) \times 6 \div (-3)$ positive or negative?
